methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $5.7B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
The Boring Company is a late-stage Transportation infrastructure firm valued at $5.7B post-money, having raised $675M to date. The Transportation sector comprises four identified private peers (Rivian, Einride, Harbinger, REGENT Craft) and zero public comparables, limiting the breadth of available valuation anchors.

Stage-progression analysis, the sole applicable triangulation method, applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier to the $5.7B last post-money valuation, yielding an implied value of $6.8B. This multiplier reflects typical valuation expansion from late-stage to subsequent financing rounds. Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable: while four Transportation peers exist, none satisfy the criteria for narrow peer comparison (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier), and drawing on the full sector median would introduce unwarranted heterogeneity.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.8B to $7.9B, with a central estimate of $6.8B. This narrow range—derived from a single method—reflects a methodological constraint rather than convergent confidence. Key uncertainties include the appropriate expansion multiple for late-stage private progression (assumed 1.2x but potentially volatile), the absence of same-stage Transportation peer data, and limited visibility into company-specific unit economics relative to the peer set. A more robust triangulation would require either expansion of the comparable universe or disclosure of financial metrics enabling income-based approaches.
